ref: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
text: Inyi s…Ы n…Ы …Фmahohoe ka nyia ka m…Ы…Ы, ∆Жf…ФfuiћГa GbangbaћГ …Фkp…Ы …Фsha k…Фm…Ы …Фs…Ы …Ф…ЦavuћГm…Ы t…ЫzazaћГ n…ЫћА ebui gagaliћГ m…ЫbaћА d…Ы m…Ф.
translated: If someone wants to do something, the chief Gbangban will help him so that his plans will succeed.

ref: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
text: ∆Жm…Фs…Ы …Ыg…Ы y…Фl…Ф ebui…Ы petee g…Ы Owusoso Akaa at…Ф…Ц…Ф t…Ы…Ц…Ф…Ц…Ф tse…Цe w…Фn…Ф.
translated: They say that there is a big festival in Owusoso Akaa every year.

ref: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.

translated: If someone wants to buy a goat, they must first ask the elders before making a decision.

ref: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
text: N…Ы w…Фn…Ф m…Ы tsy…Ы…Ы, ban…Ф abal…ФћБ p…Ы babaћАs…Ы as…Ыn…Ы t…ЫћА bad…Фkpas…Ыan…Ы etsyina t…Фn…Ф al…Ы.
translated: If you see him, tell his father that he should come to the meeting tomorrow morning.

ref: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
text: вАЬOtsi…Ы n…Ф, …Ы…Ыl…Фk…ФћА w…Фn…Ф …Фkp…Ы ah…Ф…Ы ak…Ф Owusoso Akaa n…ЫћА y…Ыibui…Ы g…Ы al…Фk…Ф ebhui b…Фl…Ф ebu…Ф n…ФћА. K…Ыl…Ы baћАbh…Ф w…Фn…Ф wunu kabha p…Ы k…ЫbaћАk…Ф w…Фn…Ф bag…Ы badal…Ы w…Фn…Ф shu…Ф petee ka≈ЛaћГsi…Ы.
translated: Yesterday, elders from the village went to Owusoso Akaa to discuss the issues that have been troubling the community. They agreed to meet again next week to find a lasting solution to the problems.

ref: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, вАЬMore blessings come from giving than from receiving.вАЭ
text: N…Ы k…Ыka nyia ka g…Ы …Ыbh…Ыt…Ы m…Ы…Ы, …Ыkas…Ы w…Фn…Ф …Фs…ЫћА k…Ыx…ФћГ…Ы gagaliћГ …Ыl…Ы n…Ыidi w…Фaw…Фa m…Ы k…Ыl…Ы …Фs…Ы b…Фpl…Ыn…Ф bag…Ы ohumi …Фma bal…Ы ah…Ф…Ы, …Ы…ЫbakaћА ebui al…Ы g…Ы Apalete Yesu a…Ц…Ф as…Ы, вАШTiyuyulaћА kpas…Ы k…Ыd…ФћГ k…Фk…Ф m…Ы …Фd…Ы k…Ыd…ФћГ y…Ыy…Ыk…Ы m…Ы.вАЩ вАЭ
translated: If someone slaps you on one cheek, do not retaliate, but rather offer the other cheek as well, just as our Lord Jesus Christ said, вАШIf someone strikes you on one cheek, turn the other to them also.вАЩ

ref: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
text: Beyiadz…Ы tal…Ы g…Ы bakazana any…Ы azaћГ y…Ыah…Ф…Ы g…Ы ba…Ц…Ф ebui eluћБ.
translated: Children who do not listen to their parents often get into trouble.

ref: But Paul answered, вАЬWhy are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!вАЭ
text: K…Ыl…Ы Paulo ebuiliuluћГ as…Ы, вАЬKi…Цe sop…Ы n…ФobiyoyoћА p…Ы n…Фy…Ы…Ы m…Ы kituћБ…Ф ana? G…Ыn…Ы m…Ы t…ЫnyanyaћГ ete shu m…Ы bal…Ы …Фk…Ф, p…Ф …Ыbal…Ы tsy…Ы s…Ы t…ЫћА itsi n…Ы Yerusalem n…Ы Apalete Yesu y…Ыinye…Ы n…Ыtakpu.вАЭ
translated: But Paul answered, вАЬWhat are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but even to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.вАЭ

ref: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
text: Bad…Фkpas…Ыan…Ы teli g…Ыede Kaisarean…Ф agbana b…Фl…Ф av…Ыn…Ф ediku Mnason g…Ыenu ad…Фkpas…Ыan…Ы tenikpo y…Ы…ФpaћГ m…Ы p…Ыb…ФzaћГ …Ыk…Ыl…Ы. Enu an…Ф g…Ыede Kipro p…Ы edzi ad…Фkpas…Ыn…Ы g…Ыiput…ФћА tenikpo. Paulo Yerusalem Tikuku
translated: The disciples from Caesarea brought us to the house of Mnason, where we were to stay. He was a man from Cyprus and one of the early disciples. Paul went to Jerusalem with us.

ref: They were shouting, вАЬFriends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.вАЭ
text: p…Ы bakp…Ы oziћГ as…Ы, вАЬIsraelan…Ы, n…Фpl…Ыn…Ф …Фl…Ф m…Ф! Any…Ы g…Ы akas…Ы ban…Ф n…Ф petee b…Фd…ФћГ n…Ы …Фk…Ф nyia …Фk…Ф afalan…Ф p…Ы al…Ф…ЫtitiћБ n…Ы b…Фl…Ф banun…Ф n…ЫћА …Фshaa shu n…ЫћА …Ыk…Ыl…Ы tsy…Ы n…Ы al…Ы. N…Ы k…Ыl…ЫћА niede…Ы, ad…Ыgbana Helan…Ы amana AkaavuћГ…Ф shu p…Ы akp…Ы …Фdadal…Ы …Фk…Фl…ЫћА b…Фts…ФћГ.вАЭ
translated: But the Lord said, вАЬIsraelites, do not be afraid! I have rescued you from the hand of your enemies and I will bring you to the land that I promised to your ancestors. There you will live in peace, and your children and grandchildren will prosper.вАЭ

ref: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
text: G…Ы bap…Ы as…Ы t…ЫћА beyi y…Ы, ebui al…Ы ediku keyuad…Ыd…Ы g…Ы agbana Roman…Ы keyuizaa nat…ФћГ m…Ы …Фs…Ы kititiћА l…Фl…Ф…Ы ebui eku Yerusalem …ФmaћГ m…Ы.
translated: But when the time came, they took him and brought him before the Roman governor because they said he was causing trouble in Jerusalem.

ref: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
text: Tsi…ФћГ …Фl…Ы ko…Ф, k…Ыl…Ы agbana …ЫzaћГk…Ф banun…Ф n…ЫћА buyuad…Ыd…Ы teli p…Ы bebu t…Ыts…Ы…Ц…Ы m…Ы av…Ы ban…Ф…Ыzaa m…Ы. Kibe…Ы g…Ы ban…Ф…Ыza am…Ф keyuiza otugbaћА an…Ф n…ФћА n…ЫћА y…ЫћА buyuad…Ыd…Ы, bay…Фl…Ф Paulo huisiћА.
translated: In the evening, when everyone had gathered together, they sat and talked about the things that had happened during the day. Among those who spoke about what they had seen and heard was Paulo himself.

ref: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
text: Banun…Ф teli n…Ы ban…Ф…Ыza m…Ы akp…Ы oziћГ a…Ц…Ф kibui …Ыl…ЫћА, k…Ыl…Ы beteli tsy…Ы a…Ц…Ф k…Ыbhlat…Ф, k…Ыl…Ы g…Ы keyuiza otugbaћА an…Ф n…ФћА ag…Ыm…Ф kibui…Ы n…Ыtakpu n…Ы oziћГ…Ы g…Ы loyiћА…Ы so…Ф, ak…Ф …Фsha as…Ы bagbana Paulo av…Ыn…Ф buyuad…Ыd…Ы …ФzaћГk…Ф…Ф.
translated: The child was not with his mother at home, but he was with his father, and when the news reached the mother that her child was not at home, she quickly went to the house where Paul was staying to look for him.

ref: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
text: G…Ы Paulo ediku geduluћГ…Ф, kana g…Ы banun…Ф bam…Ы ataћГ kifu…Ф ebewuћА kabha so…Ф, k…Ыl…Ы …Фs…Ы buyuad…Ыd…Ы abhasaћА y…Ы ewun…Ф kabha.
translated: When Paul was about to die, he called his children and told them to always do good things, so that people would remember them after their death.

ref: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
text: G…Ы beluћГ y…Ыa…Ц…Фa…Ц…Ф …Фk…Ф al…Ы n…Ы Aramaik buluhuћГ…Ф m…Ы so…Ф, bad…Ф kananaћБ. K…Ыl…Ы Paulo a…Ц…Ф as…Ы,
translated: If you look carefully at the Aramaic language, it is different. But Paul said that,

ref: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
text: вАЬYudaeyi m…Ыinu, bam…ФћБ m…Ы n…Ы Tarso n…Ы Kilikia, p…Ф Yerusalem …Фmal…Ы m…Ы bany…Фn…Ф m…Ы. Gamaliel y…Ыad…Фkpas…Ыn…Ы m…ЫidziћА p…Ы …Ыkpas…Ы b…Фl…Ф akaal…Ы …Фshaa piapii k…Ыl…Ы …Ыz…Ыwl…Фak…Ы …Фk…Ф Owusoso Akaa as…Ы kana g…Ы w…Фn…Ф …Фmay…Ыm…Ы aka nyia aka abh…Ыt…Ы …Ыl…Ы obu…Ф ana.
translated: вАЬI am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in Jerusalem. I was educated at the feet of Gamaliel according to the strict manner of the law of our ancestors, being zealous for God just as all of you are today.вАЭ

ref: I asked, вАЬLord, what do you want me to do?вАЭ Then he told me, вАЬGet up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.вАЭ
text: Ibisi s…Ы, вАЬ вАШApalete, ki…Цe k…Ыl…Ы …Фs…Ы m…Ыbh…Ыt…Ы?вАЩ Apalete…Ы etse…Цe m…Ыas…Ы, вАЬ вАШL…Ф…ЫtitiћБ t…ЫћА …Фv…Ы Damasko …Фmam…Ы. N…Ы …Ыk…Ыl…Ы bebeћАtse…Цe w…Ф ebui…Ы g…Ы bakp…Ы w…Фah…Ф…Ы …Фs…Ы …Фbh…Ыt…Ы…Ы.вАЩ
translated: 'Ibisi said, "вАШApalete, where are you going?вАЩ Apalete answered him, "вАШI am going to Damascus. I have been sent to bring back those who are there.вАЩ"

ref: Then Ananias told me, вАЬThe God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
text: вАЬK…Ыl…Ы etse…Цe m…Ы as…Ы, вАШB…Фl…Ф akaal…Ы Owusoso Akaa y…ЫebuћБ w…Ф …Фs…Ы t…Ы otiћА k…Ыg…Ы y…Ыap…Ы n…Ф, …Фs…Ы t…ЫћА …Фm…Ф An…Ф Dadal…Ы, n…ЫћА t…Ы oluћГ ebui eden…Ф y…ЫolugbuћГ m…ЫћА.
translated: So they said to me, вАШGo and tell Owusoso Akaa that if he comes today, he will not find us here, if he comes tomorrow, he should ask An…Ф Dadal…Ы, and if he comes the day after tomorrow, he should look for us in the bush.вАЩ

ref: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
text: W…ФbodziћА an…Ф g…Ы …Фb…Ф…Ц…Фa…Ц…Ф …Фdal…Ы y…Ыishu …Фk…Ф banun…Ф petee n…Ы k…Ыg…Ы …Фm…Ф p…Ы oluћГ…Ф shu.
translated: They wake up early in the morning and go to the farm before the sun rises so that they can finish their work early.

ref: of the Lord who said to me, вАЬHurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.вАЭ
text: k…Ыl…Ы …Ыm…Ф Apalete…Ы y…Ыa…Ц…Фa…Ц…Ф etse…Цe m…Ы. A…Ц…Ф as…Ы вАШH…Фa! Sh…Ы n…Ы Yerusalem otsi…Ы tsi…Ф, …Фm…Фs…Ы bat…Ы bay…Ыk…Ы w…Ф…Ыbui…Ы g…Ы …Фb…Ф…Ц…Ф n…Ы m…Ыishu…Ф.вАЩ
translated: Then Apalete…Ы looked around. He said, вАШLook! When Jerusalem is surrounded by armies, you will know that its destruction is near.вАЩ

ref: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.вАЭ
text: K…Ыl…Ы kibe…Ы g…Ы bazabal…ЫћА Stefano g…Ыetsi n…Ы w…Ф…Ыtakpu dzyosu n…Ф, …ЫzaћГ …Ыk…Ыl…Ы p…Ы …Ыz…Ыkp…Ы bal…Ы kemi p…Ы izipo bag…Ы eyi y…Ы akp…Ыkp…Ы n…Ф.вАЩ
translated: But when they were stoning Stephen, he prayed, вАШLord Jesus, receive my spirit.вАЩ

ref: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
text: keyuiza an…Ф gbagbla ak…Ф …Фsha as…Ы bagbanaћА Paulo av…Ыn…Ф buyuad…Ыd…Ы …ФzaћГk…Ф…Ф. Akas…Ы al…Ы as…Ы behuisi y…Ы t…ЫћА bebisi y…Ы, t…Ы betiћА kibui n…Фn…Ф shu sop…Ы banun…Ф akp…Ы oziћГ …Фl…Ы n…Ы y…Ыabha ana.
translated: So then, after many days had passed, Paul said to the brothers, вАЬLet us go back and visit the believers in every town where we preached the word of the Lord, and see how they are doing.вАЭ

ref: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
text: Bag…Ы ap…Ы as…Ы ebisi y…Ы ebui…Ы, beyideћА odunu …Фl…Ы. KifuћА bayiћБ keyuiza an…Ф gbagbla tsy…Ы g…Ы ebetiћА as…Ы anyaћГ Paulo g…Ыenu Romaeyi…Ы akp…Ы …Ы…Цahui…Ы m…Ы. Paulo N…Ы ∆Жham…Ы BeshesheћА…Ы Nam…Ы
translated: If someone wants to hear this story, they should listen carefully. Today we will talk about how Paul traveled to Rome. Paul Faces Many Challenges

ref: Paul turned to the high priest and said, вАЬYou whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.вАЭ
text: K…Ыl…Ы Paulo etse…Цe y…Ыas…Ы, вАЬOwusoso Akaa ebehuw…Ф ebho, w…Ф kigiliћА g…Ы badzaћГ b…Фd…ФћГ fufuћБ ak…Ф! ∆ЖzaћГ esiћА n…Ы …Ыk…Ыl…Ы p…Ы okulu kebhu n…Ы m…Ы n…Ы …Фsha nuluћГ, p…Ф w…Фtsy…Ы …Фg…Ы …Фbh…Ыt…Ы t…Ф…Ц…Ф …Фsha nabha g…Ы …Ф…Ц…Ф …Фs…Ы behu m…Ы k…Ыd…ФћГ!вАЭ
translated: But Paul shouted loudly, вАЬDonвАЩt harm yourself, for we are all here! Then he called for a light, rushed in and trembling with fear, he fell down before Paul and Silas!вАЭ

ref: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
text: G…Ы a…Ц…Ф kibui …Ыl…Ы ko…Ф, kawl…ФћГak…Ы ebeku Farisian…Ы n…ЫћА Zadukian…Ы n…Ыishi, k…Ыl…Ы kazaћГ ebebu …Фt…Ф n…Ы bag…Фl…Ф shu.
translated: Some Pharisees and Sadducees came to him to test him.

ref: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, вАЬWe have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
text: Bav…Ы ban…ФluћГazazaћГ gbagbla n…ЫћА beshesheћА…Ы …Фk…Ф editse…Цe bal…Ы as…Ы, вАЬB…ФtanaћА b…Фd…ФћГ …Фs…Ы, b…Фt…Ы baћА≈ЛaћГ b…Фd…ФћГ nanaћА odiyin…Ф …Фs…Ы buyiћА Paulo.
translated: When the children finished playing, they sat down and listened as he said, вАЬIf you do good, you will become like the wise man called Paulo.вАЭ

ref: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.вАЭ
text: Kliso otsi…Ыn…Ф, w…Фn…Ф n…ЫћА …Фham…Ы beshesheћА…Ы …Фk…Ф …Фsha ganaћА keyuiza an…Ф gbagbla nabha t…ЫћА abh…Ыt…Ы as…Ы n…Фk…Фp…Ы kibui m…Ы dadal…Ы n…Ы y…Ыibui…Ы g…Ы abh…Ыt…Ы shu. B…Фl…Ф x…Фn…Ф b…Фbal…Ы s…Ы b…ФћАba yii flogo p…Ы ebekuћА …Ыk…Ыl…Ы.вАЭ
translated: When the sun rises, people go to their farms to work so that they can get food for their families and themselves to eat. Every evening they return home with what they have harvested.

ref: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, вАЬWhat do you want to tell me?вАЭ
text: Keyuiza an…Ф gbagbla anyanaћГ any…Ы nuv…Ф…Ы n…Ф kah…Фkp…Ф, agbana y…Ыav…Ыn…Ф k…Ыw…Фa p…Ы edibisi y…Ыas…Ы, вАЬKibui …Цuwoe w…Фp…Ы …Фs…Ы t…ЫћА otse…Цe m…Ы?вАЭ
translated: When the elders were discussing important matters in the meeting house, the chief stood up and asked, вАЬWhat should we do about this problem that has come up?вАЭ

ref: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.вАЭ
text: N…Фp…Ы …Ыs…Ф …Фk…Ф Paulo tsy…Ы t…ЫћА azaћГ y…Ыabha as…Ы t…ЫћА bagbana y…Ыav…Ыn…Ф Felike g…Ыenu k…Ыmadi ekusi…Ы n…Ы ishu y…Фy…Ф m…Ы.вАЭ
translated: But when Paul had decided to travel, he passed through Macedonia and spoke encouraging words to the people in those places, then he went to Greece.

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Yudan…Ы akp…Ы olugbuћГ n…Ы ebui…Ы g…Ы bal…Фk…Ф ebhui y…ЫuluћГ…Ф m…Ы, k…Ыl…Ы beetuil…Ы akp…Ы as…Ы ebui…Ы al…Ы enu ebui gbaћГ.
translated: If someone does not listen to advice and keeps doing what they want, then when trouble comes, they will face it alone.

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, вАЬI will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.вАЭ
text: K…Ыl…Ы Felike an…Ф g…Ыenyi Kidzo …Ыl…Ы piapii n…Ф, al…Фk…Ф kebhu…Ф at…Ф…Ц…Ф kiwui bhlat…Ф abha. A…Ц…Ф as…Ы, вАЬOnup…Ы Lisia g…Ыenu ag…Ы y…ЫagbanaћА buyuad…Ыd…Ы aba eden…Ф Yerusalemu…Ф tsy…Ы, …ЫbaћА…Цi kibhui…Ы g…Ы …Ыbabhui w…Ф…Ыbui…Ы m…Ы.вАЭ
translated: When Felix heard the case, he ordered the soldiers to keep Paul under guard but to give him some freedom and allow his friends to take care of his needs. He said, вАЬThe commander Lysias will come down here from Jerusalem, and I will decide your case then.вАЭ

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: Akp…Ы keyuad…Ыd…Ы an…Ф gbagbla …Фsha as…Ы t…ЫћА ak…Ф bazaћГ epoћБ Paulo piapii, p…Ф ak…Ф y…ЫazaћГ y…Ыishu ah…Ф…Ы m…Ы t…ЫћА ak…Ф y…Ыbhia al…Ы ebe…Цi y…Ыabha n…ЫћА k…Ыg…Ы y…Ыap…Ы…Ы n…Ф.
translated: Thank you very much for yesterday evening when you came to PauloвАЩs house, we were very happy to see you and to talk with you about what we discussed.

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ewui gelee ede…Ы, Felike n…ЫћА y…ЫedziniћА Drusila g…Ыenu Yudadzi…Ы aba. Akp…Ы ban…Ф n…Ы Paulo …Фk…Ф k…Ыl…Ы azataat…ФћГ niy…Ы kibe…Ы g…Ы aza…Ц…Фa…Ц…Ф n…Ы t…Ыy…Ыy…Ыk…Ы≈ЛaћГ g…Ыl…Ы Yesu Kristo…Ф m…Ы shu…Ф.

Ebui kpa bhlat…Ф enu awuhuћГ dzadzaћГ n…Ы ban…Ф shu, ban…Ф yiyiћБ, ebla p…Ыp…Ы, ban…Ф w…Ф…Ыw…ФћА…Ы n…ЫћА …Фhagbo shu tiziziћГ. Benu olugbuћГedzi agagaћГ an…Ф,
translated: If you plant corn and beans together, they grow well, and when they are ripe, you harvest them, cook them and eat them. Beans also help the soil to be fertile so that corn grows well, grows big, and produces well, and the corn will be healthy. This is how our ancestors used to farm.

ref: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
text: ka…Ц…Ф kpa …Ц…Ф…Ц…Ф n…Ы ban…Ф shu, ban…Ф g…Ы anyana Owusoso Akaa akp…Ы …Ыya m…Ы, ban…Ф g…Ы bak…Фala an…Ф, befeke ishu p…Ы beku ishu aw…ФћГ, bap…Ы…Ы edzo n…Фn…Ф abha g…Ы babagaћГ abh…Ыt…Ы ibui ziziћГ, bat…Ыkp…Ы …Ыtakpu esi ak…Ф bal…Ы akaa n…ЫћА bal…Ы eliћГ al…Ы,
translated: If you want to do something good, do it like Owusoso Akaa did, do it with all your heart, let your hands and feet work together, let your words be true and your actions be honest, let your mind be clean and your thoughts be pure, so that your name will be remembered for good.

ref: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
text: Kliso…Ф w…Ф…Ыtakpu k…Фk…Ф nanaћА ma, w…Ф anun…Ф g…Ы okulu ebhu …Фk…Ф an…Ф teli…Ы, …Фm…Фs…Ы kibui n…Фn…ФћБ idi g…Ы …Фl…Фk…Ф okulu ebhu …Фk…Ф an…Ф bhlat…Ф n…Ф…Ф, k…Ыl…Ы w…Ф tsy…Ы …Фl…Фk…Ф obu ishui ip…Ф n…Ф, …Фm…Фs…Ы w…Фan…Ф g…Ы obhui ibui m…Ы tsy…Ы…Ы, …Фbh…Ыt…Ы kibui …Ыl…Ы n…Ыidi.
translated: If a child is going to school in the morning, he should eat food before going to school in the morning, but if he does not eat at home, he should be given food to eat at school, so that he will not be hungry.

ref: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
text: Owusoso Akaa a…Ц…Ф as…Ы, вАЬebebhui ibui m…Ы ak…Ф an…Ф nyia an…Ф n…Ы k…Ыg…Ы abh…Ыt…Ы nuluћГ.вАЭ (Psalomo
translated: The Lord says, "Call on me in times of trouble and I will deliver you."

ref: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
text: P…Ф bag…Ы bap…Ы bal…Ыy…Ы ete t…Ыp…Ыp…Ы, p…Ы bash…Ы n…Ы ah…Ф…Ы n…Ы kibui g…Ыl…Ы …Ыl…Ы …ФzaћГk…Ф shu p…Ы bal…Ы ebui kpa ede…Ы, bat…ФћГ p…Фp…ФћГ gagaliћГi n…ЫћА …Фm…Фp…Ф…Ы …Фl…Ы …Фk…Ф al…Ы.
translated: If someone wants to do good things, they should not wait for others to tell them, but they should do it themselves and help those who are in need.

ref: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
text: p…Ф ishu dadal…Ы, bobo n…ЫћА ishu y…Фy…Ф …Фk…Ф an…Ф nyia an…Ф g…Ы abh…Ыt…Ы ibui gbangbaћГ. Ban…Ф g…Ы eput…Ф…Ф enu Yudan…Ы, flogo p…Ы …Фk…Ф bag…Ы betinu Yudan…Ы tsy…Ы.
translated: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples sat down to eat. While they were eating, he said, вАЬTruly I tell you, one of you will betray me.вАЭ

ref: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
text: p…Ф n…Ы bal…Ы t…ЫgagaћГ n…Ы …Фsha luћГ…Ф…Ф, k…Ыkas…Ы …Фs…Ы baf…Фn…Ф …Фsha n…Ы bal…Ы etuћБ…Ф m…Ы, k…Ыl…Ы bal…Ы etuћБ…Ф m…Ы ebui tsy…Ы adal…Ы al…Ы shu, k…Ыl…Ы bal…Ы ekpe…Цekpe…Цe…Ы m…Ы ebhui al…Ы uluћГ, k…Ыl…Ы kidzo …Ыl…Ы nabhaa y…Ыebu bal…ЫuluћГ tsy…Ы.)
translated: If someone does something bad to you, do not do the same bad thing to them, but rather do good to them so that they may change, and if they still do not change, leave them to God, because God knows how to deal with everyone.
